36	// ReadinessClassificationResult is the deterministic bridge between the
37	// bead-lifecycle readiness vocabulary and work scheduling semantics.
38	type ReadinessClassificationResult struct {
39		Classification       string
40		Reason               string
41		SystemReason         string
42		TriageClassification string
43		IntakeOutcome        PreClaimIntakeOutcome
44	}
45	
46	// ClassifyReadiness maps bead-lifecycle readiness evidence to a stable result.
47	// Infrastructure signals always win over bead-quality signals so routing,
48	// quota, transport, resource, and repository-concurrency failures do not park a
49	// bead as needs_refine/needs_split.
50	func ClassifyReadiness(classification string, reasons []string, detail string) ReadinessClassificationResult {
51		return ClassifyReadinessWithMode(classification, reasons, detail, config.BeadQualityModeWarnOnly)
52	}
53	
54	// ClassifyReadinessWithMode maps bead-lifecycle readiness evidence to a stable
55	// result using the configured bead-quality mode.
56	func ClassifyReadinessWithMode(classification string, reasons []string, detail, qualityMode string) ReadinessClassificationResult {
57		classification = normalizeReadinessClassification(classification)
58		reason := firstReadinessReason(reasons)
59		systemReason := classifyReadinessSystemReason(detail, reasons)
60		if classification == ReadinessClassificationSystemUnready ||
61			reason == ReadinessReasonSystemUnready ||
62			systemReason != "" {
63			if systemReason == "" {
64				systemReason = ReadinessSystemReasonUnavailable
65			}
66			return ReadinessClassificationResult{
67				Classification:       ReadinessClassificationSystemUnready,
68				Reason:               ReadinessReasonSystemUnready,
69				SystemReason:         systemReason,
70				TriageClassification: triageClassificationForSystemReason(systemReason),
71				IntakeOutcome:        PreClaimIntakeError,
72			}
73		}
74	
75		if fromReason := readinessClassificationForReason(reason); fromReason != "" {
76			classification = fromReason
77		}
78		if classification == "" {
79			classification = ReadinessClassificationReady
80		}
81	
82		out := ReadinessClassificationResult{
83			Classification: classification,
84			Reason:         reason,
85		}
86		switch classification {
87		case ReadinessClassificationReady:
88			out.IntakeOutcome = PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic
89		case ReadinessClassificationNeedsSplit:
90			if out.Reason == "" {
91				out.Reason = ReadinessReasonTooLarge
92			}
93			out.IntakeOutcome = PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed
94		case ReadinessClassificationOperatorRequired:
95			if out.Reason == "" {
96				out.Reason = ReadinessReasonAmbiguousScope
97			}
98			out.IntakeOutcome = PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired
99		case ReadinessClassificationNeedsRefine:
100			if isReadinessBlockingMode(qualityMode) {
101				// In BLOCK/factory mode, a refinement need still parks the bead.
102				out.IntakeOutcome = PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired
103			} else {
104				// WARN-ONLY mode reports the finding but still proceeds.
105				out.IntakeOutcome = PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic
106			}
107		default:
108			out.Classification = ReadinessClassificationSystemUnready
109			out.Reason = ReadinessReasonSystemUnready
110			out.SystemReason = ReadinessSystemReasonUnavailable
111			out.TriageClassification = triageClassificationForSystemReason(out.SystemReason)
112			out.IntakeOutcome = PreClaimIntakeError
113		}
114		return out
115	}

126	func normalizeReadinessClassification(classification string) string {

133			return ReadinessClassificationNeedsSplit
134		case "operator_required", "human_review_required", "ambiguous":
135			return ReadinessClassificationOperatorRequired

16	func compileReadinessChecksSchema(t *testing.T) *jsonschema.Schema {
17		t.Helper()
18	
19		_, currentFile, _, ok := runtime.Caller(0)
20		require.True(t, ok)
21	
22		schemaPath := filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(currentFile), "schema", "readiness-checks.schema.json")
23		raw, err := os.ReadFile(schemaPath)
24		require.NoError(t, err)
25	
26		compiler := jsonschema.NewCompiler()
27		require.NoError(t, compiler.AddResource("readiness-checks.schema.json", strings.NewReader(string(raw))))
28	
29		// Compile also asserts the schema document itself is well-formed.
30		schema, err := compiler.Compile("readiness-checks.schema.json")
31		require.NoError(t, err)
32		return schema
33	}
34	
35	// TestReadinessChecksSchema locks the readiness payload shape behind the shared
36	// readiness-checks.schema.json so the producer prompt and the Go decoder cannot
37	// drift the way the parent wedge (ddx-f9ddaa68) did, where the producer emitted
38	// readiness_checks[].verdict as a JSON bool while the consumer parsed a string.
39	//
40	// Every verdict form readinessVerdict.UnmarshalJSON
41	// (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:91-121) accepts must both
42	//   - validate against the schema (AC1/AC4: tightening the schema to reject any
43	//     accepted form fails this test), and
44	//   - decode to the canonical readinessVerdict (AC2: pass/fail/passthrough/empty).
45	func TestReadinessChecksSchema(t *testing.T) {
46		schema := compileReadinessChecksSchema(t)
47	
48		// buildPayload returns a full readiness payload whose single readiness_checks
49		// entry carries the given raw JSON for verdict, or omits verdict when empty.
50		buildPayload := func(verdictJSON string) string {
51			entry := `{"reason":"missing_verification","evidence":"AC lacks go test","checkable_before_attempt":true`
52			if verdictJSON != "" {
53				entry += `,"verdict":` + verdictJSON
54			}
55			entry += `}`
56			return `{"classification":"needs_refine","rationale":"check","readiness_checks":[` + entry + `]}`
57		}
58	
59		cases := []struct {
60			name        string
61			verdictJSON string
62			want        readinessVerdict
63		}{
64			{name: "bool_true_to_pass", verdictJSON: `true`, want: "pass"},
65			{name: "bool_false_to_fail", verdictJSON: `false`, want: "fail"},
66			{name: "string_fail_passthrough", verdictJSON: `"fail"`, want: "fail"},
67			{name: "string_ready_passthrough", verdictJSON: `"ready"`, want: "ready"},
68			{name: "string_not_ready_passthrough", verdictJSON: `"not_ready"`, want: "not_ready"},
69			{name: "null_empty", verdictJSON: `null`, want: ""},
70			{name: "absent_empty", verdictJSON: "", want: ""},
71		}
72	
73		for _, tc := range cases {
74			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
75				payload := buildPayload(tc.verdictJSON)
76	
77				// AC1/AC4: the schema must accept every form the decoder accepts.
78				var v any
79				require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(payload), &v))
80				require.NoError(t, schema.Validate(v),
81					"verdict form %q must validate against readiness-checks.schema.json", tc.verdictJSON)
82	
83				// AC2: the same form must decode to the canonical readinessVerdict.
84				var classified preClaimReadinessClassificationPromptResult
85				require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(payload), &classified))
86				require.Len(t, classified.ReadinessChecks.Checks, 1)
87				assert.Equal(t, tc.want, classified.ReadinessChecks.Checks[0].Verdict)
88			})
89		}
90	
91		t.Run("malformed_kind_rejected", func(t *testing.T) {
92			payload := buildPayload(`{"kind":"pass"}`)
93	
94			var v any
95			require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(payload), &v))
96			require.Error(t, schema.Validate(v), "object verdicts must be rejected by readiness-checks.schema.json")
97		})
98	}

+		return "object"
+	case '[':
+		return "array"
+	case '"':
+		return "string"
+	case 't', 'f':
+		return "bool"
+	case 'n':
+		return "null"
+	default:
+		return "number"
 	}
 }

+func TestReadinessVerdict_AcceptsBoolStringOrNull(t *testing.T) {
+	cases := []struct {

+		wantErr string

+		{name: "string_trimmed_uppercase", payload: `"  PASS  "`, want: "pass"},
+		{name: "null", payload: `null`, want: ""},

+	}
+
+	for _, tc := range cases {
+		t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
+			var verdict readinessVerdict

+			if tc.wantErr != "" {

+				assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), tc.wantErr)
+				return
+			}
+			require.NoError(t, err)
+			assert.Equal(t, tc.want, verdict)
+		})
+	}
